theme.title: Direct and Indirect Objects
theme.description: Practice direct object pronouns, indirect object pronouns, and combinations of both in practical Spanish.

lesson.title: Lesson 2: Indirect Objects
lesson.instructions: Practice me, te, le, nos, and les with common giving and telling verbs.

exercises.prompt: I give my friend a gift.
exercises.answer: Le doy un regalo a mi amigo.
exercises.explanation: 'Le' marks the person receiving the gift.

exercises.prompt: Mi madre me prepara cafe todas las mananas.
exercises.answer: My mother makes coffee for me every morning.
exercises.answer_alt: My mother prepares coffee for me every morning.

exercises.prompt: Can you tell us the truth?
exercises.explanation: The indirect object pronoun can attach to the infinitive or go before the conjugated verb.

exercises.prompt: Les escribo a mis abuelos cada semana.
exercises.answer: I write to my grandparents every week.

exercises.prompt: The teacher explains the exercise to us.
exercises.answer: La profesora nos explica el ejercicio.

exercises.prompt: No le voy a contar eso.
exercises.answer: I am not going to tell him that.
exercises.answer_alt: I am not going to tell her that.

exercises.prompt: They are sending me a message right now.
exercises.answer: Me estan mandando un mensaje ahora mismo.
exercises.answer_alt: Estan mandandome un mensaje ahora mismo.
exercises.explanation: The receiver takes the indirect object pronoun.

exercises.answer: I always recommend this restaurant to you.
exercises.explanation: 'Te' marks the person receiving the recommendation.
